title: Women's History Month, 2026
summary: Proclamation 11017 of March 12, 2026, declares March 2026 as Womens History Month, celebrating the contributions of American women throughout history and in the present. The proclamation highlights women's roles in strengthening families, enriching culture, pioneering industries, and shaping the nation's future. It also outlines the administration's policies aimed at empowering women, such as expanding the child tax credit, creating Trump Accounts, reducing medicine costs through TrumpRx, enforcing Title IX, preserving scholarships for female athletes, and restoring public safety. The text also mentions economic initiatives like a 20 percent tax deduction for small businesses, new trade markets for farmers, and the elimination of taxes on tips and overtime pay through the One Big Beautiful Bill, all intended to foster greater success for women in the workforce and economy. The proclamation calls for public officials, educators, and all citizens to observe Womens History Month with appropriate programs and activities.
